title: Fix librarian.py wikilink path resolution (repo-root-relative and own-dir-relative)
description: Follow-on to TASK-174: fixes a real resolution bug the librarian-batch2 helper agent diagnosed while applying wikilinks to a second batch of docs. add_wikilinks_to_related_section() only resolved bullet paths relative to MAP_System/ root, so subdirectory docs using (a) repo-root-relative paths with an explicit MAP_System/ prefix, or (b) bare filenames relative to the referencing file's own directory, were all silently skipped as unresolved (10 of 12 batch-2 files got 0 conversions before this fix, 2 after). Added _resolve_related_bullet_path() trying multiple bases in the correct order; Guidelines/-prefixed refs are deliberately left unresolved since that corpus is outside this tool's validate/backlinks scope. Re-ran the fixed tool against all 12 batch-2 files: 44 wikilinks now convert cleanly, 0 broken/ambiguous. Read map-library-viability-measurement-results-2026-07-14.md and the librarian-batch2 helper's hcom diagnosis before editing further.
acceptance_criteria: _resolve_related_bullet_path() correctly resolves repo-root-relative (MAP_System/-prefixed) and referencing-file's-own-dir-relative bullet paths, in addition to the existing root-relative case.
Guidelines/-prefixed references are deliberately left unresolved (documented, not a bug) since that corpus is outside validate/backlinks' default scope.
Re-running the tool against the 12 batch-2 files converts substantially more bullets than before the fix (was 2 total, now 44), with librarian.py validate reporting 0 broken/ambiguous links.
Focused regression tests cover both new resolution bases and the Guidelines/ exclusion.
